A Rezoning Amendment and Draft Plan of Subdivision application to re-develop the site with 6 new residential buildings,  a community centre, a public park, public roads and private driveways.  The buildings would range in height from 4 storeys to 25 storeys and contain a total of 941 new dwelling units consisting of townhouses and apartment units of which 341 are TCHC units and 600 are market units.  The units would consist of townhouses and apartment units.  The existing 152 TCHC apartment units at 5 Needle Firway would be retained.  Building One would contain 123 TCHC units, Building Two would contain 218 TCHC units, Building Three would contain a community centre and daycare facilities, Building Four would contain 283 market units, Building Five would contain 48 market units, Building 7a would contain 142 market units and Building 7b would contain 127 market units.   A total of 667 parking spaces would be provided site wide.Properties include:  3, 36 Marsh Grassway, 7, 11 Blue Grassway, 4, 17 Cane Grassway, 5, 10, 40 Turf Grassway and 1,2, & 8 Dune Grassway.

To permit a mixed use development that would include one north residential building (stepping up to 15-storeys (47.2 metres)) and one south residential building (stepping up to 17-storeys (53.1 metres)) connected by a podium (4-storeys (12.75 metres)) as well as one office building (8-storeys (32.1 metres)). The proposal would include a total gross floor area of 53,072 square metres, of which 43,736 square metres would be for residential space, 7,730 square metres for office space, 1,225 square metres for retail use, and 372 square metres (381 square metres including the vestibule) for community agency space. This site would contain a total of 535 new residential units, of which 525 would be condominium units and 10 would be Affordable Ownership units. A total of 358 vehicular parking spaces are proposed within a 4-level above grade parking garage that would be located along the rail corridor. In addition, a 868 square metres new public park is proposed.

Redevelopment comprising of four mixed-use buildings ranging from 8 to 37-storeys. Revised Application (August 2021) : The revised submission has reduced the tower heights of each tower by 2 storeys, to 25 and 29 storeys (previously 27 and 31 storeys), 483 residential units, 522 vehicular and 393 bike parking spaces, 1 type G and 2, type C loading spaces, and an FSI of 5.0 times, in response to City staff comments. See related Site Plan Application No. 21 198274 ESC 20 SA.Original Application: Bousfields Inc. has submitted an application for a Zoning By-law Amendment to permit 2 new residential buildings in addition to the two existing apartment buildings on the subject property. The proposed buildings would be located in the area currently occupied by an existing 4-storey parking structure, which would be demolished. The proposed residential towers of 27 storeys (west tower) and 31 storeys (east tower), would be located above a 6-storey building base (podium). The proposal would have a total of 520 residential units comprising of 200 units in the west tower; 240 units in the east tower; and 80 units in the 6-storey building base. The proposed gross floor area of the new buildings (64, 664 square metres) and the existing residential buildings (28,548 square metres) would result in a Floor Space Index of 5.5 times the lot. A total of 882 vehicular parking spaces, 422 bicycle parking spaces, 1 type-G and 1 type-C loading spaces would be provided to service the proposed development

5 and 43 Junction Road The revised application seeks approval for the redevelopment of 5 and 43 Junction Road, proposing the construction of six residential towers with heights ranging from 15 to 35 storeys. These towers will feature mid-rise podiums that accommodate residential, commercial, and office spaces. The total gross floor area (GFA) of the site will be 135,767.76 square meters, comprising 126,453.36 square meters of residential GFA and 9,314.40 square meters of non-residential GFA.
